mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals monetary tightening that raises equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising FEDFUNDS, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets that boost corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite falling UNRATE, or t-test p > 0.05 for return differentials.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust consumer demand that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST shows Rank IC < 0.05, or 60d returns are negative in > 40% of periods with concurrent growth.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates weakening supply chains that depress corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite falling INDPRO, or t-test p > 0.1 for return differentials.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in GDP (GDPC1) signals strong macroeconomic momentum that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60d forward returns are negative in > 35% of periods with GDP growth, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising CPI (CPIAUCSL) indicates inflationary pressures that force central banks to tighten policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite rising CPI, or t-test p > 0.05 for return differentials.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates a resilient labor market that supports consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20d forward returns are negative in > 40% of periods with PAYEMS growth, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding supply capacity that lowers inflation expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite rising INDPRO, or t-test p > 0.1 for return differentials.
